cycleNotes: Cycle 5 re-confirmed all 26 patterns with fresh signal evidence. work tracking tool notification triage hits 85 total occurrences — strongest single pattern. Eval ecosystem patterns (eval-results, eval-template, partner-enablement) collectively represent 61 occurrences and 22.5 hrs/wk — the largest pattern cluster. Meeting-notes-action-item-capture now at 45 occurrences across all 4 sources. Feature Flags-rollout-runbook-assistant declining for 3rd consecutive cycle — archived candidate. New insight: recurring-meeting-agenda-generator confirmed across 8 distinct weekly meetings with full stability metadata.

patterns.label: Azure DevOps Notification Triage
patterns.llmRationale: 85 work tracking tool notification emails across 5 cycles (25 new this cycle) covering Evals, InfraGate, Teams Premium, Workstream D, Workstream E, and Copilot workstreams. Subject patterns are machine-parseable ([Eval], [AI Eval], [Workstream E] prefixes + Requirement/Task/Bug/Epic type). ~80% require no human action. Present in every cycle — the single most consistent signal in the entire catalog. AutomationScore raised to 92 due to 5-cycle confirmation of rule-based classificability.

patterns.label: Meeting Notes & Action Item Capture
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 5 adds 8 new occurrences: meeting-to-notes synthesis workflow (freq 3) and cross-team meeting output duplication (freq 5). SDR-from-meeting-notes template confirmed as actively shared. Pattern now at 45 occurrences across all 4 M365 source types over 5 cycles — the broadest-source pattern in the catalog. 7.5 hrs/wk of redundant meeting output creation is the second-highest time cost.

patterns.label: Agent Eval Setup, Analysis & Standardized Reporting
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 5 adds 13 new occurrences: 6 Program X/Evals strategic email threads and 7 eval methodology thought-leadership discussions in Teams. With 51 total occurrences and 10.75 hrs/wk, this is the highest-value pattern by time investment. Eval-driven development vs MVP-first, trajectory evaluation, CI/CD integration, and actionability gaps remain the recurring discussion themes. ValueScore raised to 90 — the lack of a shared eval schema is the single most impactful structural problem identified across 5 cycles.

patterns.label: VoC / Customer Ask Deduplication & Routing
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 5 adds 3 new occurrences from customer/field eval feedback routing to product (Customer Tracker, Enterprise Client A, Enterprise Client C, Enterprise Client B). 25 total across 5 cycles. Pattern spans Teams → work tracking tool → Teams follow-up loops. Retrieval-augmented dedup against work tracking tool backlog remains a strong fit. 5-cycle consistency confirms structural nature.

patterns.label: Eval Framework Template & Coverage Model Scaffolding
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 5 confirms Agent Eval Sets Template.xlsx maintenance on weekly cadence with acceptance criteria and quality bar updates. Coverage models and acceptance criteria templates still authored independently per PM. A skill generating standardized eval templates from agent description + domain eliminates parallel reinvention.

patterns.label: Action Items Tracked Across Fragmented Systems
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 5 signals reinforce cross-tool fragmentation. Action items scatter across Loop tables, Teams threads, meeting transcripts, and work tracking tool. Pattern confirmed but no new discrete occurrences beyond what is subsumed by meeting-notes and status-report patterns. Trend stabilized.

patterns.label: Weekly Project Status Report Generation
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 5 adds 4 new occurrences: cross-team status update duplication (freq 3) and Services Org Analytics PM Review Loop (freq 1). Leadership explicitly requests post-meeting status reports. Status tables manually reformatted each time. ParticipantCount raised to 4 reflecting PM, Engineering Lead, Program Manager, and Leadership involvement. work tracking tool-pull-to-Loop automation confirmed high-confidence.

patterns.label: External Stakeholder Post-Meeting Recap & Follow-Up Drafting
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 5 adds Atlas Session content-sharing coordination thread (freq 1). Pattern holds: post-meeting workflow for external engagement generates recap, file share, next-steps, follow-up scheduling. Seen across multiple partners and customer domains.

patterns.label: Meeting Transcript → Manual Summary → Loop/PPT Pipeline
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 5 adds meeting-to-notes synthesis workflow (freq 3) explicitly documenting the conversation-to-artifact transformation. Large meetings generate multiple parallel summarizations. A single canonical transcript-in / structured-summary-out skill eliminates duplication. Time tracked increased to 4.0 hrs/wk.

patterns.label: Customer Engagement Documentation: SDR & Discovery Note Creation
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 5 adds 2 new occurrences of SDR/discovery-note creation flagged as incomplete or inconsistent. Pattern confirmed across 2 cycles now. Shifting documentation between roles increases risk of parallel reinvention. A skill enforcing canonical SDR format from meeting notes + customer context addresses the inconsistency.

patterns.label: Cross-Tool Issue Thread Fragmentation & Context Loss
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 5 adds 7 new occurrences: technical artifact sharing hub (freq 5) and enablement asset duplication (freq 2). Multiple teams using different enablement decks, templates, and materials causing confusion. Consolidation is now a 7.5 hrs/wk problem across 4 source types. The widest-source fragmentation problem in the catalog.

patterns.label: Personal Daily Prep & Briefing via M365 Copilot
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 5 confirms personal daily prep page maintained at 5x/week frequency. A skill pulling from calendar, work tracking tool sprint, open action items, and prior meeting decisions can generate this briefing in seconds each morning. ParticipantCount=1 limits org-wide value but high personal productivity impact.

patterns.label: Recurring Meeting Agenda Preparation
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 5 adds 11 new meeting occurrences across 8 distinct weekly meetings: Project Alpha v-team (Mon), Learning & Scaling Fridays, Program X Camp Standup (Mon-Thu), Regional Pod Standup (Tue), Program X Architecture Call (Mon), Customer Engagement Sync (Fri), Office Hours (Thu), Tiger Team Social (Thu). Each has fixed attendees, fixed time, and fixed format. Time tracked jumped to 15.92 hrs/wk — the highest of any single pattern by time investment.

patterns.label: Partner & Customer Eval Enablement Session Coordination
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 5 confirms customer/field eval feedback routing and requests for deeper eval tooling walkthroughs. Pattern holds stable. Repetitive partner enablement content delivered per-partner in recurring meetings. Value score 88 — highest among stable patterns.

patterns.label: Calendar Overload & Meeting Invite Triage
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 5 confirms 55 meetings/week pattern (110 cumulative across 2 cycles). 64% of work week blocked. 12h30m of unresponded invites. Meeting scheduling workflow signals (freq 2) reinforce the need for calendar triage. The volume of meetings IS the meta-pattern. A triage advisor recommending accept/decline/delegate based on strategic priority could reclaim 5-10 hrs/wk.

patterns.label: Action Item Owner Chasing & Status Follow-Up
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 5 adds inbound-signal-to-meeting-scheduling workflow (freq 2) — proposing availability and sending calendar invites as a repeating pattern. PMs and TPMs consistently spend time chasing owners and scheduling follow-ups.

patterns.label: Training / Hackathon Event Artifact Coordination
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 5 adds 10 new occurrences: Loop workspace creation for programs/events (freq 2), TechCon event planning (freq 3), and technical artifact sharing hub (freq 5). Event coordination format repeats per event — strong candidate despite lower automationScore. 27.08 hrs/wk cumulative is the third-highest time investment.

patterns.label: Customer Signals Aggregation & Planning Tracker Refresh
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 5 confirms Services Org Customer Signals for Planning.xlsx as a living planning tracker with quarterly refresh cadence. Pattern confirmed across 2 cycles. Signal aggregation and prioritization via shared trackers that get refreshed rather than replaced.

patterns.label: Redundant One-Off Deck Creation for Overlapping Topics
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 5 adds 2 new occurrences from enablement asset duplication signal. Multiple teams independently create guidance with overlapping intent but scattered locations. ParticipantCount raised to 4. A skill detecting existing relevant decks and proposing merge/fork strategies prevents 6.5+ hrs/wk of duplicate content creation.

patterns.label: Access Governance & Compliance Alert Triage
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 5 adds Loop workspace compliance (freq 3) and Purview/SharePoint security alerts (freq 2). Pattern confirmed across 4 distinct alert types. Near-rule-based classification. Action-required vs informational classification is near-perfect.

patterns.label: Copilot / Agent Platform Architecture Q&A
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 5 adds 11 new occurrences across 3 Q&A categories: Agent 365/registry fundamentals (freq 5), Teams vs Copilot Chat surface parity (freq 3), agent lifecycle/migration edge cases (freq 3). User treated as source of truth. AutomationScore raised to 70 — clearer Q&A categories enable a RAG-backed FAQ skill. 33 total occurrences across 5 cycles.

patterns.label: Ownership Routing & DRI Lookup
patterns.llmRationale: No new discrete signal in cycle 5. Manual DRI coordination persists as background overhead. Chronic low-grade friction rather than accelerating problem.

patterns.label: Operational FYI Broadcast Email Classification
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 5 adds 5 new digest/Viva Engage/BIC All Hands occurrences. AutomationScore 92 but valueScore 42 — single-participant scope, minimal per-email time cost. Best deployed as a component inside broader inbox management.

patterns.label: Escalation & Follow-Up Email Drafting
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 5 confirms urgent external escalation email pattern. Low frequency but high value per instance. Tone calibration requirement keeps automationScore at 70.

patterns.label: Last-Minute Review Deck & Doc Maintenance Before ROBs
patterns.llmRationale: Cycle 5 adds Services Org Analytics PM Review Loop as a monthly cadence review doc. Pattern is periodic (monthly) — absence from weekly signals expected. PPTX-as-collaboration-medium with no upstream structured data source is the root cause.

patterns.label: Feature Flags Flights & Rollout Unblocking Guidance
patterns.llmRationale: No signal in cycles 3, 4, or 5. Three consecutive cycles without evidence. Feature Flags rollout assistance diminishing as rollout matures. Recommend archiving after cycle 6 if still absent.

candidateSkills.name: task-notification-router
candidateSkills.description: Reads Azure DevOps notification emails, classifies each by work-item type and prefix tag, sets an action-required flag, assigns a suggested routed-to role, and emits a one-line summary — filtering out the ~80% that need no human action.
triggerExamples: Triage my work tracking tool notification stream and route each item to the right owner
Which work tracking tool emails this week actually need my attention?
Classify these work tracking tool emails by type: Bug, Requirement, Customer Ask, Epic, or FYI
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Eliminates 60-90 min/week of inbox scanning across 3 roles. 85 occurrences over 5 cycles — the most consistent signal in the catalog.

candidateSkills.name: meeting-notes-action-extractor
candidateSkills.description: Ingests a meeting transcript and emits structured action items with owner/due-date/priority, decisions log, and follow-up email draft — replacing 2-3x manual duplication across Loop, Teams, and email.
triggerExamples: Extract all action items and owners from this meeting transcript
Summarize this Teams thread into a Loop page with decisions and next steps
Create an SDR from the notes of this customer engagement meeting
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Replaces 7.5 hrs/week of redundant meeting output creation across 4 roles. Broadest source coverage (all 4 M365 types).

candidateSkills.name: eval-report-synthesizer
candidateSkills.description: Ingests eval results against a template schema and produces a standardized report: pass/fail summary, step-level failure breakdown, prioritized action items, and shareable eval report.
triggerExamples: Analyze these eval results and generate a standardized report
Compare these eval results against our acceptance criteria
Generate an eval report covering trajectory and step-level failures
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Saves 10.75 hrs/week of fragmented analysis by enforcing a single eval schema across 5 roles. Highest value score (90) in the entire catalog.

candidateSkills.name: voc-ask-deduplicator
candidateSkills.description: Compares incoming Customer Ask against existing work tracking tool backlog using retrieval-augmented lookup, returns duplicate match candidates with confidence scores, and recommends DRI routing.
triggerExamples: Is this customer ask already in our work tracking tool backlog?
Find duplicates for this Customer Tracker VoC item and suggest the right DRI
Route this eval tooling feature request to the right team
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Replaces 25+ manual triage events across 5 cycles with instant deduplication and routing.

candidateSkills.name: weekly-status-report-generator
candidateSkills.description: Drafts a Loop-formatted weekly/monthly project status report by pulling from work tracking tool sprint work items, prior meeting notes, and the previous report.
triggerExamples: Draft this week's project status report from my work tracking tool sprint
Generate a Loop status report for the eval platform workstream
Create a post-meeting status summary for leadership
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Eliminates manual cross-tool aggregation for recurring status reports. Leadership request pattern confirmed.

candidateSkills.name: eval-template-scaffolder
candidateSkills.description: Generates standardized eval scenario guide, coverage model, and acceptance criteria template from agent description and domain context.
triggerExamples: Generate an eval template for a declarative agent grounded in SharePoint
Create a coverage model for a Copilot Studio agent handling HR queries
Build a reusable Agent Eval Sets template with acceptance criteria
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Eliminates 4+ hrs/week of parallel eval template authoring across 3 roles. Enables cross-team eval comparability.

candidateSkills.name: unified-action-item-tracker
candidateSkills.description: Scans across meeting notes, Loop, Teams, email, and work tracking tool to surface all action items in a unified view with dedup, ownership, and overdue flagging.
triggerExamples: What are all my open action items across all tools this week?
Find every action item from this project and tell me which are overdue
Which action items from last week's architecture call are still unresolved?
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Replaces 3.33 hrs/week of manual cross-tool reconciliation across 4 roles.

candidateSkills.name: recurring-agenda-generator
candidateSkills.description: Pre-populates agendas for recurring meetings by pulling from work tracking tool open items, prior meeting blockers, and action item status. Covers 8 distinct weekly meetings.
triggerExamples: Generate the agenda for this week's Project Alpha v-team meeting
Pre-populate the Program X Architecture Call agenda from open blockers
What should we cover in Friday's Customer Engagement Sync?
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Reduces 15.92 hrs/week of meeting prep across 6 participants and 8 recurring meetings. Highest time investment of any single pattern.

candidateSkills.name: copilot-platform-faq-responder
candidateSkills.description: RAG-backed FAQ skill answering recurring Agent 365 governance, Teams vs Copilot Chat parity, and agent lifecycle/migration questions.
triggerExamples: What is Agent 365 vs other agent types?
Why does my agent behave differently in Teams vs Copilot Chat?
How do I migrate an agent without breaking existing connections?
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Deflects 33 recurring Q&A instances across 5 cycles, reclaiming 6.92 hrs/week of expert time.

candidateSkills.name: knowledge-context-consolidator
candidateSkills.description: Consolidates fragmented information across Teams threads, email, documents, and meeting notes into a single unified context view per topic.
triggerExamples: Consolidate everything we know about this customer engagement into one view
Find all related discussions about eval tooling across Teams, email, and docs
What enablement materials exist on this topic and where are the duplicates?
candidateSkills.valueProposition: Addresses 7.5 hrs/week of context fragmentation across 4 source types and 4 participants.
